C09000008 8/19/1988 August 19, 1988 Note From Strobe Talbott To Steve

State Department FOIA record C09000008, dated 8/19/1988, released in the July 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "August 19, 1988 Note From Strobe Talbott To Steve". The released text begins: "The hardest part is still to come -— how take the general thesis outlined here and apply it to the agenda and schedule of the Summit itself, and how we apply it to the hard choices we have ahead of us, especially vis-a-vis vis-à-vis Treasu… Wednesday, August 19, 1998, 7 a.m. • What we need: a "high concept" paper that puts the objectives of Summjt in the context of our long-term (and long-standing) the Summit strategy toward Russia, and that balances the economic agenda POTUS…"

C09000036 12/29/1991 December 29, 1991 Note From Strobe Talbott to MKA

State Department FOIA record C09000036, dated 12/29/1991, released in the Sep 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 29, 1991 Note From Strobe Talbott to MKA". The released text begins: "They confirm what had been my assumption anyway: the central allegation of the piece is utter, mischievous nonsense; and I was in the dark by inadvertence. They also agree that I should have been read into this matter in a timely way, both because of my responsibilities in D and also because of my more specific duties on Russia policy."

C09000054 4/28/1993 Proposed Message to President Yeltsin

State Department FOIA record C09000054, dated 4/28/1993, released in the July 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "Proposed Message to President Yeltsin". The released text begins: "I have also attached a proposed response to Yeltsin (which I have reviewed with Strobe Talbott). The proposed message includes our views on a variety of the most important issues on the agenda with Russia."

State Department FOIA record C09000053, dated 7/29/1993, released in the July 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "July 29, 1993 Note From Strobe Talbott To Tony Lake". C09000058 5/2/1994 YOUR APPEARANCE ON CNN TOMORROW

State Department FOIA record C09000058, dated 5/2/1994, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "YOUR APPEARANCE ON CNN TOMORROW". The released text begins: "You can use the occasion to assert our objectives, explain our strategy, review our accomplishments, and push back against our critics. C09000054 11/7/1994 Note dated November 7, 1994 from Strobe Talbott to Boss

State Department FOIA record C09000054, dated 11/7/1994, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "Note dated November 7, 1994 from Strobe Talbott to Boss". The released text begins: "I'll do it only when I think the importance of the subject at hand requires it -- and when I feel 1'd be letting you down in some sense to leave the rnatter to "the system." C09000052 12/12/1994 December 12, 1994 letter from Strobe Talbott to Yuri

State Department FOIA record C09000052, dated 12/12/1994, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 12, 1994 letter from Strobe Talbott to Yuri". The released text begins: "Like the last such letter I wrote you, quite a while ago, this is an entirely private communication. C09000003 8/5/1995 August 5, 1995 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Jaswant

State Department FOIA record C09000003, dated 8/5/1995, released in the Sep 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "August 5, 1995 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Jaswant". The released text begins: "Three generations of our family are gathering, under the of my father (an ornithological pandit and master of leadership of of the wilderness), for an expedition to the Galapagos Islands. Before departing, I wanted to put before you some thoughts about our next meeting."

C09000034 9/25/1995 September 25, 1995 letter From Strobe Talbott To Elise Lewis with attachment

State Department FOIA record C09000034, dated 9/25/1995, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "September 25, 1995 letter From Strobe Talbott To Elise Lewis with attachment". The released text begins: "Lewis: I am writing in enthusiastic support of Tom Donilon for Council membership. Please take this letter as a genuinely personal endorsement from someone who knows the Council well -- and of someone who, I'm convinced, would make a real contribution to the activities of the CFR for a long time to come."

C09000064 2/16/1996 February 16, 1996 Note From Strobe To Sandy W/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000064, dated 2/16/1996, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "February 16, 1996 Note From Strobe To Sandy W/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "We need to capitalize on the opportunity that the Preval inauguration gives us to turn a corner, both respect to our influence on the Haitian government and to the partisan contention over our policy on the Hill. Much of the animosity to our policy was personalized in terms of Aristide, and it was manifest in skepticism about whether Aristide would step down according to the Constitution."

C09000066 2/16/1996 February 16, 1996 Note From Strobe To Sandy W/Attachment(s)

The released text begins: "As you know, Joan Spero has felt that our current system is a bit out of kilter. I've been exploring the idea of setting up a somewhat more inclusive review process within State, with Joan officially added to the mix so that commercial/economic factors are assured more focused consideration."

C09000006 3/22/1996 March 22, 1996 Letter From Acting Secretary To Elaine Agnes McGovern

State Department FOIA record C09000006, dated 3/22/1996, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "March 22, 1996 Letter From Acting Secretary To Elaine Agnes McGovern". The released text begins: "McGovern: l'm writing to convey my regret and apologies for the confusion at the Retirement Ceremony this morning. 0ur reliance on computer lists can and does get us in trouble, as it did today. I did want to congratulate you on a job well done in a series of difficult posts around the world: Belgrade, Pretoria, Rabat, Nairobi, Libreville, New Delhi, Colombo and, fiiially, Vienna."

C09000009 3/29/1996 March 29, 1996 Letter From Deputy Secretary To Hans von Ploetz

State Department FOIA record C09000009, dated 3/29/1996, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "March 29, 1996 Letter From Deputy Secretary To Hans von Ploetz". The released text begins: "In my view, there is a clear need to move beyond the agreed text of Initial Elements to a discussion of how to give the new arrangement some real teeth in controlling sensitive exports. I understand, however, that Germany may wish to revisit some issues worked out in the text of the Initial Elements with a view towards reducing commitments for information sharing and other transparency measures."

C09000028 10/27/1996 October 27, 1996 Note From Strobe Talbott To Chris

State Department FOIA record C09000028, dated 10/27/1996, released in the Apr 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "October 27, 1996 Note From Strobe Talbott To Chris". The released text begins: "As always, I'm very appreciative of the time and care you devoted to the task of keeping me out of trouble, in this case by going over my Harriman Institute speech. I think you have a very valid point: the tone did get a bit "non-governmental." Perhaps a synonym would be sassy or even (perish the thought!) journalistic (you were kind enough to call it merely academic)."

State Department FOIA record C09000019, dated 10/29/1996, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "October 29, 1996 letter from John M. Shalikashvili to Strobe Talbott". The released text begins: "C. 20318-9999 V51rSf'ff 46 The Honorable Strobe Talbott Deputy Secretary of State 2201 C Street, NW Washington, D.C. 20520 Dear Strobe, How kind of you to provide a copy of "Democracy and the National Interest" prior to its publication in… It is regrettable when the guiding importance of democracy in our foreign policies has become debatable and requires a defense, but yours is as eloquent and persuasive as any I've read."

State Department FOIA record C09000030, dated 12/4/1996, released in the Apr 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 4, 1996 Note FROM STROBE TALBOTT, IN OSLO TO THE SECRETARY OF STATE - ALL COPIES TO THE SECRETARY". The released text begins: "Except for Albania, this the only country in Europe I've never visited before. C09000031 12/20/1996 December 20, 1996 letter from Strobe Talbott to Admiral John Shkor

State Department FOIA record C09000031, dated 12/20/1996, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 20, 1996 letter from Strobe Talbott to Admiral John Shkor". The released text begins: "Our morning at the JIATF gave us an acute and vivid appreciation of the important work you and your colleagues are doing on behalf of our nation's counter-narcotics program. It's inspiring to see our armed forces at work on the front line of one of the most serious challenges we face in the post-Cold War era."

C09000033 12/26/1996 December 26, 1996 letter from Strobe Talbott to L. Ronald Scheman with attachments

State Department FOIA record C09000033, dated 12/26/1996, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 26, 1996 letter from Strobe Talbott to L. Ronald Scheman with attachments". The released text begins: "Your emphasis on the importance of resources and markets accurately articulates a crucial element of the President's policy in forging a new hemispheric relationship. While there are many challenges ahead, we remain committed to the principle of free trade in the Americas and the many initiatives that originated in the 1994 Miami Summit of the Americas."

The released text begins: "The 105th Congress will in all likelihood be asked to ratify the first of NATO's accession treaties with Central European nations. Consequently, now is the time to establish a high degree of contact and coordination between the executive and legislative branches."

The released text begins: "I wanted you to know that I called Bob Carswell earlier this week and asked him to strike my name from consideration for the Carnegie Endowment presidency. The released text begins: "They reaffirmed their commitment to the shared goal of building a stable, secure, integrated and undivided democratic Europe. The roles of the United States and Russia as powers with worldwide responsibilities place upon them a special requirement to cooperate closely to this end."

The released text begins: "It suggested that one reason the tobacco companies went ·for the deal was that they stand to make such immense profits from overseas sales, especially in Asia, that it's worth agreeing to this package in order to get the USG off their back. C09000041 7/1/1997 July 1, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Livia Sylva w/Attachment(s)

The released text begins: "I.want to assure you that I share your admiration for the remarkable achievements Romania has made, especially under its new government, and I understand how strong the desire is among the Romanian people to join NATO. However, the President has decided, after extensive consideration of many factors, that the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland were the strongest candidates."

The released text begins: "The Geneva conventions and other treaties establish the limits of warfare so as to protect non-combatants, be they civilians, the wounded or war prisoners. This body of law also defines war crimes (grave breaches and other violations), and obliges parties to arrest and try alleged war criminals."

C09000062 11/3/1997 November 3, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Peter G. Peterson w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000062, dated 11/3/1997, released in the Jan 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "November 3, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Peter G. Peterson w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "The idea of a Mediterranean Regional Commission to address issues such as the environment and immigrant concerns is a good one, as is the collateral idea of inviting mayors of key cities in the region to participate in such a commission. We feel this overall approach should be encouraged."

C09000058 11/4/1997 November 4, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To John J. Hamre w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000058, dated 11/4/1997, released in the Jan 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "November 4, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To John J. Hamre w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "With the NEI, we aim (a) to help the Baltic countries become stronger candidates for integration into western institutions, (b) to expand cooperation between northern Europe and northern Russia as part of our broader effort to encourage Ru… The foreign ministers of the five Nordic and three Baltic countries enthusiastically welcomed this initiative and are now proposing new ways of visible cooperation with the United States to meet NEI objectives."

C09000027 12/3/1997 Handwritten Note w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000027, dated 12/3/1997, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Handwritten Note w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "The current debate over NATO's role in Bosnia, and its implications for the Alliance's future, however, leads me to hazard a few comments based upon my peacekeeping and NATO experience. The Alliance's essential function, it seems to me, is to link American power to European security, counterbalancing that of Russia, and thereby maintaining an equilibrium among Europe's major powers."

C09000027 12/11/1997 December 11, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Igor

State Department FOIA record C09000027, dated 12/11/1997, released in the Jan 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 11, 1997 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Igor". The released text begins: "If the word "partnership" means anything — which I firmly believe it does! — 1 have certainly found that it applies to my working relationship with you. So does another (alas, over-used) word: friendship. ln no small measure thanks to you, the U.S. and the Russia worked well together at the Bonn PIC. including in "calling things by their own names." when the Serbs walked Out."

C09000023 12/12/1997 ACTING SECRETARY'S LETTER TO FOREIGN SECRETARY COOK ON SUMMIT FOLLOW-UP AND AGRICULTURAL TRADE ISSUES

State Department FOIA record C09000023, dated 12/12/1997, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "ACTING SECRETARY'S LETTER TO FOREIGN SECRETARY COOK ON SUMMIT FOLLOW-UP AND AGRICULTURAL TRADE ISSUES". The released text begins: "Ambassador should seek to deliver to Foreign Secretary Robin Cook at the earliest opportunity the text of letter from Acting Secretary Talbott relating to our trade concerns on the eve of the UK's EU Presidency (para. 4). The letter thanks the UK for its active efforts to work with us in advance of its presidency on the New Transatlantic Agenda (NTA) ."

C09000037 12/13/1997 December 13, 1997 Note From Strobe Talbott To John Holum W/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000037, dated 12/13/1997, released in the Sep 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 13, 1997 Note From Strobe Talbott To John Holum W/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "As you can imagine, she's been going 500 miles a minute, but I'll try to get an okay from her this week. • I look forward to my meeting with our colleagues, and of course I'll make the points you suggest. • Let's definitely try to sit down… I'll be especially interested to hear about your lunch with Slocombe and Hamre on the subject. • Attached is letter I've received from Jim Goodby asking to be considered for one of a number of jobs, including START III negotiator."

C09000066 12/27/1997 December 27, 1997 Note From Strobe Talbott To A/S Roth

State Department FOIA record C09000066, dated 12/27/1997, released in the Sep 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 27, 1997 Note From Strobe Talbott To A/S Roth". The released text begins: "You've a splendid job since you've come on board in establishing intellectual and presentational mastery of your vast and diverse area of responsibility. C09000034 1/7/1998 Continuing Our Dialogue on Russia Policy

State Department FOIA record C09000034, dated 1/7/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "Continuing Our Dialogue on Russia Policy". The released text begins: "Craigv SUBJECT: Continuing Our Dialogue on Russia Policy I agree with your opening point: Our principal differences are rooted more in terminology and typology than in prescription and policy. Putting things in categories has been a human failing since Noah's day -- who knows how many wonderful species he forgot about or left behind -- and I plead guilty to inviting controversy by dividing priorities into first and second tiers."

C09000058 1/9/1998 Preliminary Analysis of Draft Implementing Regulations for the Russian Religion Law w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000058, dated 1/9/1998, released in the Oct 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "Preliminary Analysis of Draft Implementing Regulations for the Russian Religion Law w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "We have shared this paper with Representatives Chris Smith, Frank Wolf and Tony Hall, who will be in Moscow next week to meet with Russian officials who are reviewing these drafts. We are also including the analysis in a telegram to Embassy Moscow for its use in the coming weeks, when we expect the regulations to be finalized."

C09000056 1/15/1998 January 15, 1998 Note To Sandy Berger

State Department FOIA record C09000056, dated 1/15/1998, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "January 15, 1998 Note To Sandy Berger". The released text begins: "I'm doing so because I've heard and read enough from Frank on his talks in Moscow, and thought enough about the whole issue, to have reached a judgment in my own mind about what we should — and what we should not — do next. I want to put these thoughts before you because our session with Frank will, no doubt, intensify the debate that is already 7 underway among our colleagues, and I don't know when I'll have a chance privately to give you my thinking."

C09000055 3/6/1998 Weekly Report from the Department of State

State Department FOIA record C09000055, dated 3/6/1998, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Weekly Report from the Department of State". The released text begins: "KEY DEPARTMENT NEWS ■ --(e)-- Iraq: The State Department is seeking to clarify the specific modalities governing the "special group" that will accompany UNSCOM on its inspections of sites in Iraq. We expect UNSCOM to test Iraq's readiness to permit immediate and unconditional access in accordance with the Annan-Aziz inspection agreement."

C09000001 3/7/1998 CONGRATULATORY LETTER TO LITHUANIAN PRIME MINISTER ON WILLIAMS INVESTMENT

State Department FOIA record C09000001, dated 3/7/1998, released in the Dec 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "CONGRATULATORY LETTER TO LITHUANIAN PRIME MINISTER ON WILLIAMS INVESTMENT". The released text begins: "EMBASSY IS INSTRUCTED TO DELIVER THE TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING LETTER FROM THE DEPUTY SECRETARY TO PRIME MINISTER VAGNORIUS AT THE EARLIEST POSSIBLE TIME. PRIME MINISTER: LET ME CONGRATULATE YOUR GOVERNMENT ON ITS RECENT AGREEMENT WITH WILLIAMS INTERNATIONAL COMPANY FOR INVESTMENT IN AND OPERATION OF SEVERAL OIL AND GAS INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS IN LITHUANIA."

Let me start with an assertion of policy."

C09000117 3/25/1998 March 25, 1998 Letter From Strobe Talbott To James Rosappe

State Department FOIA record C09000117, dated 3/25/1998, released in the Oct 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "March 25, 1998 Letter From Strobe Talbott To James Rosappe". The released text begins: "I was pleased with every element of the visit, from my nleetings meetings with President Constantinescu and Foreign Minister Plesu to the university address. Our visit was an important element in building regional support for U.S. strategy in Kosovo and reaffirnling reaffirming Romania's steady progress toward integration in the full range of European and transatlantic institutions."

C09000119 3/25/1998 March 25, 1998 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Avis Bohlen

State Department FOIA record C09000119, dated 3/25/1998, released in the Oct 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "March 25, 1998 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Avis Bohlen". The released text begins: "I realize visits by senior officials place special demands on an embassy, and ours was no exception. You did a masterly job of ensuring our all-too-short time in Sofia was put to maximum good effect."

C09000120 3/25/1998 March 25, 1998 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Frances Cook

State Department FOIA record C09000120, dated 3/25/1998, released in the Oct 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "March 25, 1998 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Frances Cook". The released text begins: "The whole visit, from our initial conversation riding in from the airport to your send-off dinner, dinner. was just terrific. You and your team, from Jerry Feierstein on down, could not have been more knowledgeable and gracious hosts."

C09000080 5/29/1998 Memorandum to the President on UNDCP Contribution W/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000080, dated 5/29/1998, released in the Sep 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "Memorandum to the President on UNDCP Contribution W/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "I've been alerted to a potentially serious problem by Craig Johnstone, who does splendidly in the thankless task of making sure that we've got the resources to fund our policies. It's in that context that I want to flag a concern: if POTUS were to announce that we're increasing our UNDCP contribution, we don't see a way to make good on that commitment."

The released text begins: "Indeed, it's more than that: the P0TUS- Yeltsin connection has had a salutary and, on several occasions, perhaps even decisive effect on Russian internal politics. The released text begins: "Turns out, the precipitating event — the development that caused Treasury tu task State for Embassy Moscow's analysis of what Was happening in Russia — was Stalin's refusal to join the World Bank and the IIIIF. So the Cold War in a sense began with Russia's isolating itself from the institutions on which it !WIN depends for the survival of reform, NI ith all the implications that has for tlie direction of the post-CW era."

C09000088 8/23/1998 August 23, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Ms. Shocas

State Department FOIA record C09000088, dated 8/23/1998, released in the Sep 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "August 23, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Ms. Shocas". The released text begins: "Shocas, COS From Strobe Talbott TaIbott Elaine, I've discussed with Ivith the Secretary what IA I see as a good opportunity for her to make a major address both on Russia and on economics at the US-Russia Business Council in Chicago on Oct… I very much need to know whether Irvhether that fits her schedule for purposes of my own planning, since if she doesn't do it, I'll be giving that speech."

C09000090 8/23/1998 August 23, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Sandy Berger and Jim Steinberg

State Department FOIA record C09000090, dated 8/23/1998, released in the Sep 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "August 23, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Sandy Berger and Jim Steinberg". The released text begins: "Turns out, the precipitating event -— the development that caused Treasury to task State for Embassy Moscow's analysis of what was happening in Russia -— was Stalin's refusal to join the World Bank and the IMF. So the Cold War in a sense began with Russia's isolating itself from the institutions on which it now depends for the survival of reform, with all the implications that has for the direction of the post-CW era."

C09000029 9/2/1998 Kosovo DC Discussion on Use of Force; September 4; 2:00 pm; the White House

State Department FOIA record C09000029, dated 9/2/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "Kosovo DC Discussion on Use of Force; September 4; 2:00 pm; the White House". The released text begins: "In our judgement, without a credible threat of force in Kosovo, it is unlikely that the international community will be able to leverage the kind of concessions out of Milosevic that are needed to come to closure on a political settlement. The uncertainty that surrounds NATO's role in Kosovo is severely limiting the influence that NATO planning exerts on the political track and damaging the credibility of the Alliance."

The released text begins: "The Situation Despite the skillful and brave diplomatic efforts of Chris Hill, Jim O'Brien, and others, the last three months have not gone well for us in Kosovo. In the negotiations they have slowly but steadily moved forward."

C09000031 9/25/1998 YOUR RUSSIA SPEECH

State Department FOIA record C09000031, dated 9/25/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "YOUR RUSSIA SPEECH". The released text begins: "The timing of the speech is auspicious because we are only now beginning to get a somewhat clearer sense of the composition and direction of the Primakov government. You'll also be speaking five days before the nationwide strike that the Communists have called for October 7th, which represents the first major test for the yeltsin-Primakov government."

C09000011 9/29/1998 September 29, 1998 Letter To Yuri

State Department FOIA record C09000011, dated 9/29/1998, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "September 29, 1998 Letter To Yuri". The released text begins: "Dear Yuri: As we discussed, I'm writing with my personal sense of what it will take for us to make enough progress in CFE soon enough to ensure the viability of the treaty and also to do us good on other fronts. Since you and I talked about this at the Waldorf last Monday, just before Igor Sergeyevich and Madeleine had their dinner, I've been reconnoitering with my various colleagues, including Steve, Carlos and John Holum, but particularly Craig…"

State Department FOIA record C09000012, dated 10/1/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "KOSOVO: KEEPING THE RUSSIANS IN THE LOOP". The released text begins: "Reasons: 1.5 (B, D). 1. 4-9.).- I want to make sure that we are doing everything possible to ensure that over the next week or so, as we move toward possible NATO military action in Kosovo, we keep the Russians informed and, to the extent… From what we know of views and politics in the current environment here, it is certain that some Russians are laying the groundwork to push for a rupture with NATO and to a certain extent with the U.S., if use of necessary force gives them…"

State Department FOIA record C09000044, dated 10/7/1998, released in the Apr 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Weekly Report from the Department of State". The released text begins: "KEY DEPARTMENT NEWS • (U) Kosovo: UN Secretary General Kofi Annan issued a report October 5 on the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia's (FRY) compliance with UN Security Council Resolutions 1160 and 1199 related to Kosovo. C09000019 10/15/1998 Loan to Support the HEU Agreement

State Department FOIA record C09000019, dated 10/15/1998, released in the Jan 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "Loan to Support the HEU Agreement". The released text begins: "The highest levels of the Russian government have made clear that Russia will cease implementing the HEU agreement if this problem is not resolved now. We have a solution - legislative language that the Administration and Senator Domenici have agreed upon - that would allow the U.S. to loan money to Russia against the natural uranium already in the U.S."

The released text begins: "A number of people in the embassy, including Bob Pearson and Mike Parmley, sang your praises. Mike said there's never been a better desk officer for that post."

C09000060 10/26/1998 Russia In Search of Realism

State Department FOIA record C09000060, dated 10/26/1998, released in the Oct 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "Russia In Search of Realism". The released text begins: "The NCSJ is an organization that I have known and admired over many years, both inside the government and out, and it's a pleasure to speak to you today. I have always believed that those who, like the NCSJ, made human rights a central issue of the Cold War played an enormous role in bringing it to a successful conclusion."

C09000024 12/1/1998 December 1, 1998 Note From To Strobe Talbott

State Department FOIA record C09000024, dated 12/1/1998, released in the Dec 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 1, 1998 Note From To Strobe Talbott". The released text begins: "Pierre-Charles said the issue with the Alexis nomination was more "technical and administrative" than "political." He meant the parliament might well approve Alexis if he passed muster in the bicameral credentials committee. I understand separately that the committee's review of the audit of Alexis' handling of public funds should be completed by the end of next week and that Alexis is scheduled to appear before the committee on December 14."

C09000026 12/21/1998 December 21, 1998 Letter From To Yuri

State Department FOIA record C09000026, dated 12/21/1998, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "December 21, 1998 Letter From To Yuri". The released text begins: "Igor Sergeyevich and I were seated next to each other at the Bosnia Peace Implementation Conference in Madrid, so we had a bit of a chance, amidst other business, to compare impressions. As you can imagine, these past few days since I returned from Moscow and Madrid have been pretty hectic, hence the delay in my getting back to you with some initial reactions to the ideas I heard in Moscow about CFE."

The released text begins: "This would create conditions for international community for contacts with Yugoslav leadership to get their consent for the implementation of the international International Peace Plan. C09000018 7/17/1999 Baltic Partnership Commission w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000018, dated 7/17/1999, released in the July 2023 FOIA Library release, is titled "Baltic Partnership Commission w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "Your meeting with the three Foreign Ministers on Thursday night, along with the work we did in the Commission on Friday, reassured them that we have not forgotten them or shifted our priorities as a result of Kosovo. We agreed it was time to re- energize our cooperation and come up with a concrete work plan to take our cooperation "to the next level" for the remainder of the Administration and beyond."

C09000043 5/15/2000 Berger NMD/ABM/START Script for Meeting with President Putin w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000043, dated 5/15/2000, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "Berger NMD/ABM/START Script for Meeting with President Putin w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "DECLASSIFIED Berqer NMD/ABM/START Script for Meeting with President Putin Overview • Want to speak frankly with you about missile defense, the ABM Treaty and START. • President knows these are difficult issues for you; difficult for us all. Why the U.S. is pursuing this course • Begin by congratulating you on getting START II ratified."

C09000004 5/25/2000 May 25, 2000 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Sandy Berger w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000004, dated 5/25/2000, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "May 25, 2000 Letter From Strobe Talbott To Sandy Berger w/Attachment(s)". The released text begins: "Sandy, I'm just wrapping up an intense, potentially promising, but far- from-conclusive round with Mamedov on our Path Forward, including the all-important legal issue. Mamedov last night (Wednesday) reported directly and privately to Putin, who Yuri describes as cautiously and tentatively receptive to our approach."

C09000044 5/25/2000 When Would Activity Associated with the Construction of an ABM Rader at Shemya Violate the ABM Treaty?

State Department FOIA record C09000044, dated 5/25/2000, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "When Would Activity Associated with the Construction of an ABM Rader at Shemya Violate the ABM Treaty?". The released text begins: "Article III prohibits deployment of ABM systems and components, except at specified locations. This was intended to provide Parties with adequate notice of a possible break-out."

C09000005 5/27/2000 Basic Elements of a START III Agreement

State Department FOIA record C09000005, dated 5/27/2000, released in the Mar 2025 FOIA Library release, is titled "Basic Elements of a START III Agreement". The released text begins: "Establishment, by December 31, 2007, of lower aggregate levels of 2,000-2,500 strategic nuclear warheads for each of the parties. 2. Verification provisions contained in START I and II (which will require resolving issues related to the goal of making the current START treaties unlimited in duration) and adoption of additional verification measures. 3."

The released text begins: "It's an intelligent, cogent piece, but not, in my view, a secretarial speech. C09000021 n/a Greg:

C09000049 n/a Madrid Plan

State Department FOIA record C09000049, dated n/a, released in the July 2024 FOIA Library release, is titled "Madrid Plan". The released text begins: "Allies have been discussing planning for Madrid in Brussels and the Foreign Ministers of NATO will review at Sintra this week how the schedule for the two days will be structured. C09000050 n/a Letter from Strobe Talbott to Tony

State Department FOIA record C09000050, dated n/a, released in the May 2022 FOIA Library release, is titled "Letter from Strobe Talbott to Tony". The released text begins: "Despite strong appeals from State -- reinforced, I believe, by NSC -- Secretary O'Leary has refused to add Pakistan to a trip she is making to India later this week. It will be a significant setback to our South Asian policy in general, and our nonproliferation initiative in particular, if the first cabinet officer in a long time to visit the subcontinent does not go to Pakistan."
